items.title: Ebola Vaccine Trials Could Start This Year
items.description: As Ebola cases rise in Congo and Uganda, vaccine developers are accelerating efforts to combat the rare Bundibugyo strain. IAVI CEO Mark Feinberg says a candidate vaccine could enter human trials by y

items.title: Russia Said It Captured Key Ukraine Stronghold in Donetsk Region
items.description: Russia announced the seizure of a strategic logistics hub in the Donetsk region and claimed full control of Ukraine’s Luhansk region, days after launching its deadliest attack on Kyiv since the war be

items.title: China Coast Guard Rotates Patrol Task Group East of Taiwan
items.description: China’s coast guard said a task group led by the CCGS Xiushan vessel replaced the team headed by CCGS Daishan on Saturday to continue law-enforcement patrols in waters east of Taiwan.

items.title: Energy-Hungry Asia Is Already Drawing Lessons From Iran Crisis
items.description: There is still no lasting peace deal in the Persian Gulf, but energy-hungry Asia is already drawing energy lessons from four months of war: it needs bigger buffers, a greater diversity of fossil-fuel

items.title: Venture Capital During the AI Revolution: Masters in Business with Mamoon Hamid
items.description: Barry sits down with Kleiner Perkins Partner, Mamoon Hamid. They discuss Mamoon's thoughts on the AI revolution and his approach to early AI investing.  Mamoon also breaks down how he became an early

items.title: 1ドル200円の円安シナリオ、高市氏インド訪問、W杯ピッチ外の戦い─1週間のニュース5選
items.description: マーケットで話題になったニュースから、1週間を振り返る五つのトピックを厳選して紹介します。

items.title: SQM-Codelco Venture Maps Path to Higher Lithium Output in Chile
items.description: Chilean mining companies SQM and Codelco are paving the way to boost output at their sprawling lithium partnership by more than 70%, in a long-term bet on battery demand.

items.title: ECB’s Lagarde Will Attend Next Week’s Ecofin Instead of Vujcic
items.description: European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde will be attending next week’s meeting of European Union finance ministers in Brussels instead of her vice president.

items.title: ECB’s Nagel Stresses Need for Vigilance at Next Rate Decision
items.description: The European Central Bank should stay watchful for inflation risks and keep its options open, Governing Council member Joachim Nagel said.

items.title: CPP Invests $1.75 Billion in EQT’s AI Buildout
items.description: Canada Pension Plan Investment Board will invest $1.75 billion to support EQT AB’s artificial intelligence infrastructure buildout, it announced Friday.

items.title: Argentina Pushes $6 Billion in Repo Maturities Past Election
items.description: Argentina’s central bank extended maturities worth $6 billion on its repurchase agreements — or repos — to ease the government debt burden going into the 2027 presidential election.

items.title: Defense Sector to Remain Dynamic: Euronext CEO Boujnah
items.description: Euronext CEO Stephane Boujnah says the defense sector will remain dynamic as Europe ramps up military spending and investors continue to back the industry. He also discusses European IPOs, capital mar

items.title: Toronto Stocks Enjoy Broad Gains After New Pipeline Proposal
items.description: Canada’s stocks benchmark climbed Friday morning after Prime Minister Mark Carney unveiled a new pipeline project to the country’s West Coast.

items.title: OPEC Output Surged in June as Hormuz Flows Jumped, Survey Shows
items.description: OPEC’s crude oil production surged last month as Persian Gulf members restored exports through the Strait of Hormuz amid a peace accord between the US and Iran, according to a Bloomberg survey.

items.title: Fed and ECB Seen Diverging in French Snapshot of War Aftermath
items.description: The Federal Reserve will still need to hike interest rates this year despite Thursday’s numbers pointing to labor-market weakness, according to two French chief economists.

items.title: GFL Environmental Weighs Take-Private Amid Interest From Buyout Firms
items.description: GFL Environmental Inc. is considering a potential take-private transaction after drawing interest from buyout firms, people familiar with the matter said.

items.title: Buddhism: China's New Soft Power Front

items.title: Carlyle Hires Banks for $400 Million India IPO of RCM Business

items.title: iCON Infrastructure to Buy Northern Fiber in Rescue Deal
items.description: Lenders of Northern Fiber Holding GmbH, a struggling German fiber-network operator formerly owned by UBS Asset Management, are finalizing the sale of the company to iCON Infrastructure to avoid a loom

items.title: Pubs to Get Short-Term World Cup Boost
